zotero.abstractNote: We consider the problem of causal inference in the presence of unobserved confounders. Recent work has shown that, under certain conditions, the causal effect can be identified using a proxy variable approach, which involves solving a Fredholm integral equation of the first kind. In this paper, we propose a kernel-based two-stage estimation procedure to solve this problem. Our approach is based on the observation that the identification condition can be formulated as a conditional moment restriction. We show that this formulation allows for a flexible, non-parametric estimation of the causal effect using kernel methods. We provide a theoretical analysis of the proposed estimator, including consistency and convergence rates. Finally, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on both synthetic and real-world datasets.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of 38th International Conference on Machine Learning
